# Argus — AI Code Review That Builds Institutional Memory

> AI-powered code review that traces dependencies, remembers incidents, and simulates failures before they ship.

Argus is an AI code review tool for engineering teams. It installs as a GitHub App and automatically reviews every pull request with a multi-pass specialist pipeline.

## Core Features

- **Multi-pass review pipeline**: 7 stages — Triage, Review, Scoring, Deep Review (4 specialists: bug hunter, security, architecture, regression), Synthesis, PR Enrichment
- **Institutional memory**: Learns from past reviews, incidents, and team decisions. Every review makes the next one smarter.
- **Architecture analysis**: Dependency graph visualization with risk scoring, choke point detection, hotspot analysis, and coupling tracking
- **Failure simulation**: Predicts what breaks before code ships by simulating failure scenarios
- **Cross-PR dependency tracking**: Traces how changes in one PR affect others
- **PR enrichment with Mermaid diagrams**: Auto-generates sequence, data flow, and dependency diagrams
- **BYOK (Bring Your Own Key)**: Use OpenAI, Anthropic, or any OpenRouter-compatible LLM provider
- **Incremental reviews**: Re-reviews updated PRs analyzing only the delta

## Pricing

- **Free**: 50 reviews/month, single reviewer persona
- **Pro ($19/mo per workspace)**: Unlimited reviews, 4-specialist deep review, custom personas, priority support

## Links

- Website: https://argus.reviews
- Documentation: https://argus.reviews/docs
- Pricing: https://argus.reviews/pricing
- Compare with alternatives: https://argus.reviews/compare
- GitHub App: https://github.com/apps/argus-eye
- Blog: https://argus.reviews/blog

## Competitors

Argus competes with CodeRabbit, Greptile, Cubic, Sourcery, Qodo, GitHub Copilot code review, Codacy, SonarQube, and Semgrep. Detailed comparisons at https://argus.reviews/compare

## Technical Details

- Installs in 60 seconds via GitHub App
- Reviews complete in under 2 minutes
- Supports all languages GitHub supports
- SOC 2 compliant — code is never stored or used for training
- Self-hosted option available for enterprise
